rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current component or its ports in AGREE expressions
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component structure from the input AADL model
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are properly scoped within their respective component types or implementations
Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers from the current component or its ports in AGREE expressions
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not use range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
